subject: Geology
title: Sedimentary Structures
description: A quick win: understand Sedimentary Structures.

hook.text: Ripple marks in a billion-year-old rock look identical to ripples on today's beach. Ancient water left its mark.

buildup.text: Sedimentary structures include ripple marks, cross-bedding, mud cracks, and graded bedding in rock layers.

discovery.text: Cross-bedding forms in dunes and deltas. Its angled layers reveal ancient wind or current direction.

twist.text: Mud cracks prove the surface dried out. Finding them in deep rock means that land was once exposed to air.

climax.text: These structures are environmental fingerprints. They recreate ancient landscapes without a single fossil.

punchline.text: Water and wind sign their work in stone.

quiz.question: What do mud cracks in rock indicate?
options: The surface was once underwater and then dried out
